001/*

003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.logging;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.text.*;
024import java.util.*;
025import java.util.function.*;
026import java.util.logging.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.*;
029
030/**
031 * LogRecord that supports lazy message formatting.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * This class extends {@link java.util.logging.LogRecord java.util.logging.LogRecord} and overrides {@link #getMessage()} to use
035 * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#f(String, Object...)} for lazy formatting.
036 * The message is only formatted when {@link #getMessage()} is actually called.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>Usage:</h5>
039 * <p>
040 * This class is used internally by {@link Logger} when logging formatted messages.
041 * The formatted message is only computed when the LogRecord's message is actually
042 * accessed (e.g., by a Handler or Formatter).
043 * </p>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Logger}
047 *    <li class='jc'>{@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#f(String, Object...)}
048 *    <li class='jc'>{@link java.util.logging.LogRecord}
049 * </ul>
050 */
051@SuppressWarnings({
052   "java:S115",  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention
053   "java:S100",  // Method names match java.util.logging.LogRecord for API compatibility
054   "java:S1192", // String literals intentionally duplicated for clarity
055   "java:S2176", // Class name intentionally matches java.util.logging.LogRecord; extends it to enrich logging with Juneau-specific fields
056   "java:S2143"  // java.util.Date required for java.util.logging compatibility and Formatter %t conversions; not a behavior-preserving java.time swap.
057})
058public class LogRecord extends java.util.logging.LogRecord {
059
060   private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;
061
062   // Key constants for format placeholders
063   private static final String KEY_date = "date";
064   private static final String KEY_source = "source";
065   private static final String KEY_logger = "logger";
066   private static final String KEY_level = "level";
067   private static final String KEY_msg = "msg";
068   private static final String KEY_thrown = "thrown";
069   private static final String KEY_timestamp = "timestamp";
070   private static final String KEY_class = "class";
071   private static final String KEY_method = "method";
072   private static final String KEY_thread = "thread";
073   private static final String KEY_threadid = "threadid";
074   private static final String KEY_exception = "exception";
075
076   private transient Supplier<Optional<StackTraceElement>> source = memoize(()->findSource());
077
078   /**
079    * Constructor.
080    *
081    * @param loggerName The logger name.
082    * @param level The log level.
083    * @param msg The message pattern (will be used as-is if args is null or empty).
084    * @param parameters The format arguments, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
085    * @param throwable The throwable, or <jk>null</jk> if none.
086    */
087   public LogRecord(String loggerName, Level level, String msg, Object[] parameters, Throwable throwable) {
088      super(level, msg);
089      setLoggerName(loggerName);
090      setParameters(isNotEmptyArray(parameters) ? parameters : null);
091      setThrown(throwable);
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Returns the log message, formatting it lazily if needed.
096    *
097    * <p>
098    * If parameters were set via {@link #setParameters(Object[])}, the message is formatted
099    * using {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils#f(String, Object...)} on first access.
100    *
101    * @return The log message, formatted if parameters are present.
102    */
103   @Override
104   public String getMessage() {
105      var m = super.getMessage();
106      var p = getParameters();
107      return isEmptyArray(p) ? m : f(m, p);
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Returns the source class name, calculating it lazily from the stack trace if not already set.
112    *
113    * @return The source class name, or <jk>null</jk> if not available.
114    */
115   @Override
116   public String getSourceClassName() {
117      if (source == null)
118         source = memoize(()->findSource());
119      return source.get().map(x -> x.getClassName()).orElse(null);
120   }
121
122   /**
123    * Returns the source method name, calculating it lazily from the stack trace if not already set.
124    *
125    * @return The source method name, or <jk>null</jk> if not available.
126    */
127   @Override
128   public String getSourceMethodName() {
129      if (source == null)
130         source = memoize(()->findSource());
131      return source.get().map(x -> x.getMethodName()).orElse(null);
132   }
133
134   @SuppressWarnings({
135      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for stack trace filtering
136   })
137   private static Optional<StackTraceElement> findSource() {
138      for (var e : new Throwable().getStackTrace()) {
139         var c = e.getClassName();
140         var m = e.getMethodName();
141         // Skip internal classes, logging classes, lambda methods/classes, and synthetic methods
142         if (eq(c, cn(LogRecord.class)) || eq(c, cn(Logger.class)) || eq(c, cn(StringUtils.class)) || eq(c, cn(Utils.class))
143            || c.startsWith("java.util.logging.")
144            || (m != null && (m.contains("lambda$") || m.startsWith("access$")))
145            || c.contains("$$Lambda$") || (c.contains("/") && c.contains("$Lambda"))) {
146            continue;
147         }
148         return opt(e);
149      }
150      return opte();
151   }
152
153   /**
154    * Formats this log record as a string using the specified format pattern.
155    *
156    * <p>
157    * Similar to how {@link java.util.logging.SimpleFormatter} formats log records, this method
158    * allows you to specify a custom format string with placeholders that are replaced with
159    * actual values from the log record.
160    *
161    * <p>
162    * The format string supports both named placeholders (e.g., <js>"{date}"</js>) and
163    * {@link java.util.Formatter Formatter}-style format specifiers (e.g., <js>"%1$tc"</js>).
164    * Named placeholders are replaced first, then the resulting string is formatted using
165    * {@link java.util.Formatter Formatter} with the following arguments:
166    * <ol>
167    *    <li><c>date</c> - The date/time as a {@link Date} object
168    *    <li><c>source</c> - The source class name and method name (e.g., "com.example.MyClass myMethod")
169    *    <li><c>logger</c> - The logger name
170    *    <li><c>level</c> - The log level
171    *    <li><c>message</c> - The formatted log message
172    *    <li><c>thrown</c> - The throwable object (if any)
173    * </ol>
174    *
175    * <h5 class='section'>Named Placeholders:</h5>
176    * <ul>
177    *    <li><js>"{date}"</js> - The date/time formatted using {@link java.util.logging.SimpleFormatter SimpleFormatter}'s default date format
178    *    <li><js>"{timestamp}"</js> - The date/time formatted as ISO-8601 (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ)
179    *    <li><js>"{class}"</js> - The source class name
180    *    <li><js>"{method}"</js> - The source method name
181    *    <li><js>"{source}"</js> - The source class name and method name (e.g., "com.example.MyClass myMethod")
182    *    <li><js>"{logger}"</js> - The logger name
183    *    <li><js>"{level}"</js> - The log level name
184    *    <li><js>"{msg}"</js> - The log message (formatted if parameters are present)
185    *    <li><js>"{thread}"</js> - The thread ID
186    *    <li><js>"{threadid}"</js> - The thread ID (alias for {thread})
187    *    <li><js>"{exception}"</js> - The exception message (if thrown)
188    *    <li><js>"{thrown}"</js> - The throwable object (if thrown)
189    * </ul>
190    *
191    * <h5 class='section'>Formatter-Style Format Specifiers:</h5>
192    * <p>
193    * You can use {@link java.util.Formatter Formatter}-style format specifiers directly in the format string.
194    * The arguments are passed in the same order as {@link java.util.logging.SimpleFormatter SimpleFormatter}:
195    * </p>
196    * <ul>
197    *    <li><js>"%1$s"</js> or <js>"%1$tc"</js> - The date/time (argument 1)
198    *    <li><js>"%2$s"</js> - The source (argument 2)
199    *    <li><js>"%3$s"</js> - The logger name (argument 3)
200    *    <li><js>"%4$s"</js> - The log level (argument 4)
201    *    <li><js>"%5$s"</js> - The log message (argument 5)
202    *    <li><js>"%6$s"</js> - The throwable and its backtrace (argument 6)
203    * </ul>
204    *
205    * <p>
206    * For date/time formatting, you can use date/time conversion characters with argument 1:
207    * </p>
208    * <ul>
209    *    <li><js>"%1$tc"</js> - Complete date and time (e.g., "Tue Mar 22 13:11:31 PDT 2011")
210    *    <li><js>"%1$tb"</js> - Month abbreviation (e.g., "Mar")
211    *    <li><js>"%1$td"</js> - Day of month (01-31)
212    *    <li><js>"%1$tY"</js> - Year (4 digits)
213    *    <li><js>"%1$tH"</js> - Hour (00-23)
214    *    <li><js>"%1$tM"</js> - Minute (00-59)
215    *    <li><js>"%1$tS"</js> - Second (00-59)
216    *    <li><js>"%1$tL"</js> - Milliseconds (000-999)
217    *    <li><js>"%1$tN"</js> - Nanoseconds (000000000-999999999, since JDK 9)
218    *    <li><js>"%1$Tp"</js> - AM/PM marker (uppercase)
219    *    <li><js>"%1$tp"</js> - AM/PM marker (lowercase)
220    * </ul>
221    *
222    * <p>
223    * The format string can also contain <js>"%n"</js> for newlines.
224    *
225    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
226    * <p class='bjava'>
227    *    LogRecord <jv>record</jv> = <jk>new</jk> LogRecord(Level.INFO, <js>"User {0} logged in"</js>, <js>"John"</js>);
228    *
229    *    <jc>// Using named placeholders</jc>
230    *    String <jv>formatted1</jv> = <jv>record</jv>.formatted(<js>"[{timestamp} {level}] {msg}"</js>);
231    *    <jc>// Result: "[2025-01-22T07:42:45.123-0500 INFO] User John logged in"</jc>
232    *
233    *    <jc>// Using Formatter-style specifiers</jc>
234    *    String <jv>formatted2</jv> = <jv>record</jv>.formatted(<js>"%4$s: %5$s [%1$tc]%n"</js>);
235    *    <jc>// Result: "INFO: User John logged in [Tue Jan 22 07:42:45 PST 2025]"</jc>
236    *
237    *    <jc>// Mixed named placeholders and Formatter specifiers</jc>
238    *    String <jv>formatted3</jv> = <jv>record</jv>.formatted(<js>"%1$tb %1$td, %1$tY {level}: {msg}%n"</js>);
239    *    <jc>// Result: "Jan 22, 2025 INFO: User John logged in"</jc>
240    * </p>
241    *
242    * @param format The format string with placeholders and/or Formatter-style format specifiers.
243    * @return The formatted string.
244    * @see java.util.logging.SimpleFormatter
245    * @see java.util.Formatter
246    */
247   @SuppressWarnings({
248      "deprecation" // Date constructor is deprecated but needed for compatibility
249   })
250   public String formatted(String format) {
251      var date = new Date(getMillis());
252      Supplier<String> sourceName = () -> getSourceClassName() + ' ' + getSourceMethodName();
253
254      Function<String, Object> resolver = key -> switch (key) {
255         case KEY_date -> "%1$s";
256         case KEY_source -> sourceName.get();  // Override default behavior since logging class doesn't handle classes outside of java.util.logging.
257         case KEY_logger -> "%3$s";
258         case KEY_level -> "%4$s";
259         case KEY_msg -> "%5$s";
260         case KEY_thrown -> "%6$s";
261         case KEY_timestamp -> new SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ").format(date);
262         case KEY_class -> getSourceClassName();
263         case KEY_method -> getSourceMethodName();
264         case KEY_thread, KEY_threadid -> s(getThreadID());
265         case KEY_exception -> opt(getThrown()).map(x -> x.getMessage()).orElse("");
266         default -> "";
267      };
268
269      return safeOptCatch(()->f(formatNamed(format, resolver), date, sourceName, getLoggerName(), getLevel(), getMessage(), getThrown()), x -> x.getLocalizedMessage()).orElse(null);
270   }
271}
